rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BERNARD P. HAGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Aproval Of Settlement Agreements Relating To Beneficial Interests Of Regina Hagan And Bernard Hagan, Jr. In The Bernard P. Hagan Trust And The I Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. By the terms of the settlement agreement, since it was not approved by 3/1/23, is this petition not now moot? The initial hearing date alone made obtaining the timely approval of the settlement agreement impossible. 2. Venue is not determined by the caption of a petition previously filed. Where is the trust administered? 3. All vested or contingent beneficiaries of a trust are required to be stated with THEIR LAST KNOWN ADDRESSES, not their attorneys' addresses. [CRC 7.902.] 4. The representative and address for St. Ignatius Parish differ on the proof of service from those shown in paragraph 36 of this petition. 5. No receipts have been filed to confirm that all pecuniary bequests and tangible personal property have been distributed, as alleged in paragraph 32. 6. The register of actions indicates that an objection to this petition was filed on 3/24/23. (And a reply filed on 3/27/23 references objections filed by William Monroe.) No courtesy copy of the objections has been received by the assigned examiner as of 5/8/23. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/23/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Pay The Fee Of The Administrators; Pay The Ordinary Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Distribute The Balance Of The Estate To The Heirs Of The Decedent And Discharge The Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it.) (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) There is no Exhibit A attached to this petition. (4) It is unclear why the accounting was filed separately from this petition. In any event, no courtesy copy of that pleading has been received yet by the assigned examiner. (5) If an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (6) A cursory review of the accounting filed on 1/18/23 indicates that well over the FDIC insured amount is being retained in one credit union account. (7) When a petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. (8) Under what authority did the co-administrator(s) distribute "$35,00" to Raymond Elliott already? (9) The petition is not properly verified (not dated). (10) Paragraph 8 of the petition does not make sense without an accounting (11) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the legal description of real property and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (12) No courtesy copy of the attorney's declaration filed on 1/19/23 has been received by the assigned examiner. (13) If the declaration did not fully comply with CRC 7.702, it will need to be supplemented. (14) When did the other co-administrator die? Who is his successor-in-interest? That person must be identified and must receive notice. (Is Raymond Elliott or his estate or his trust not entitled to some portion of the statutory compensation?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/7/23. (It appears that a fully amended petition that incorporates the accounting and seeks final distribution, not continuation of estate administration) should be filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSEANNE BERTUCELLI AS TRUSTEE OF THE I TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Aproval Of Settlement Agreements Relating To Beneficial Interests Of Regina Hagan And Bernard Hagan, Jr. In The I Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. The petition filed on 12/9/22 (but not e-filed until 12/12/22) did not have a copy of the pertinent trust instrument or the settlement agreement attached to it. Referencing papers attached to a pleading filed in a different case does not make a petition complete. 2. The petitioners' own "Reply" to their petition was just filed on 4/27/23, which was a SUPPLEMENT to their petition to include a copy of the settlement agreement executed in October 2022, as amended in March 2023 (after this petition was filed). 3. No proof of service of a Notice of Hearing of the petition AND the "Reply" that completed the petition has been filed. In any event, 4/27/23 was not at least 30 days before this hearing, so notice would have been insufficient. 4. Venue is not determined by the caption of a petition previously filed. 5. All vested or contingent beneficiaries of a trust are required to be stated with THEIR LAST KNOWN ADDRESSES, not their attorneys' addresses. [CRC 7.902.] 6. The representative and address for St. Ignatius Parish differ on the proof of service from those shown in paragraph 9 of this petition. 7. Why is Roseanne Bertucelli not listed among the parties entitled to notice? Please file the durable power of attorney empowering her agent. A fully amended petition that is complete in itself, with all pertinent supporting papers attached to it, must be filed. Courtesy copies of thep amended petition and proof of service of the amended petition must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/23/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL LARRY MCLEAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23. (No earlier date available.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that an AMENDED petition had been filed. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (3) Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities filed on 10/26/22 was incomplete at item 2 (no telephone number). Deadline is 6/23/23 for delivering to the assigned examiner endorsed filed courtesy copies of (1) the proof of publication of the amended petition, (2) the petitioner's verified supplement to his amended petition, identifying the parents and their dates of death, (3) the complete Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities service, and (4) the proof of service of the complete Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate with instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HERCULES MOSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Status Report And Petition For Termination Of Trust And Discharge Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 3) The remote appearance information accompanying the DE-120 form must include a telephone appearance by CourtCall. New notice of the continued hearing must be given. 4) Did the guardian ad litem and petitioner execute the joinder agreement prior to the beneficiary's death? If not, has the beneficiary's sub-account with the master pooled trust ever been established so as to require termination thereof? 5) Has the $103,000 bond ever been issued to the petitioner so as to require exoneration thereof? 6) It is unclear whether the petition intended to request termination of the master pooled trust in its entirety or the deceased beneficiary's sub-acccount without affecting the master pooled trust. If the master pooled trust is sought to be terminated, all other beneficiaries whose sub-accounts have been established must be identified and receive notice. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUNNAR GALVINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/05/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 03/08/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copies of the petition and declaration have been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) Item 13 of the petition: Only 8 pages were attached. 4) Attachment 9: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and their issue. ii) Anna Zikmanis must be listed. iii) Sandis Bins must be listed. 5) Notice must be given to Holly Bins, Anna Zikmanis, and Sandis Bins. 6) Is the statement in the joint will the only evidence of a transmutation of the decedent's separate property interest in the subject real property to community property? 7) Does the statement in the joint will qualify as a transmutation, despite the absence of language expressing that the characterization of the subject real property was being changed from the decedent's separate property to community property? Note: An appearance may be required on 07/05/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar if the issues are not addressed. rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAN HELEN BECKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WILLIAM LITTLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing (although it is unclear why the sole heir seeks just limited IAEA authority and Bond Services of California is not a newspaper). Status of closing the estate is set for 5/20/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 5/20/24. Appearance will be required on 5/20/24 if no petition has been filed. (A petitioner should just supplement her petition to provide missing information, not "respond" to issues raised by an examiner.)
